name: Salmon Con Ternera
recipeInstructions:
1. In a shallow pan, place the salmon fillets and add onion rings, bayleaf, peppercorns, salt, and enough water to cover the fish.
2. Bring to a boil over medium heat, then reduce heat to low and simmer for 5 minutes.
3. Remove from heat, drain, and let cool slightly.
4. Cut the steak top round into 4 serving pieces and pound to an even thickness using a meat mallet or rolling pin.
5. Place the pounded beef in an ovenproof dish with the salmon fillets on top.
6. In a small bowl, combine bread crumbs, parsley, garlic, and half of the melted butter. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
7. Spread the crumb mixture evenly over the beef steaks and place the ham slice and shrimp on top.
8. Pour the remaining melted butter over the dish.
9. Bake in a preheated oven at 400°F (200°C) for 15 minutes, or until the steak is cooked to your desired doneness.
Note: Do not rush the cooking process. Allow adequate time for the steak to cook evenly and reach the proper internal temperature.
